About Year Up United
Closing the Opportunity Divide, one employer partnership at a time.
Year Up United is a workforce development organization that connects motivated young adults to the skills, experiences, and professional networks they need to launch high-quality careers. Through an intensive, employer-aligned training model, participants complete technical and professional skills development before moving into paid corporate internships, supported throughout by Year Up United coaches and staff. The result: work-ready, skills-first talent that shows up prepared, motivated, and ready to contribute from day one.
About the Event
A morning built for the future of work.
Corporate Partner Day is Year Up United's signature employer engagement event, bringing together corporate leaders, Skills-First talent, and workforce advocates for a morning of connection, insight, and shared purpose.

Whether you're a current partner deepening your commitment or exploring what a skills-first hiring strategy can unlock, this event is designed to make the value concrete, personal, and actionable.
100+
Young adults ready to launch into internships across our NY/NJ/CT market
3 Pathways
IT, Business Operations & App Dev, across 6 specializations built to fit your teams
1 Morning
To witness the talent firsthand, hear the stories, and commit to what's next

Why It Matters
Skills-first hiring isn't a trend. It's the next competitive advantage.
Expand Your Pipeline
Access pre-trained, work-ready talent across three Career Pathways, vetted, supported, and ready to contribute from day one.
Build With Intention
Connect with peers shaping equitable workforce strategies and learn what's working across industries right now.
Make Lasting Impact
Every seat you sponsor is a career launched. Every partnership you deepen is a future transformed, including your own team's.
Skills-First. Work-Ready. Right Now.
The professionals below are Year Up United alumni, people who completed our rigorous training, stepped into corporate internships, and proved what skills-first talent can do. They are not exceptions. They are the standard. Come meet the next cohort on April 15.
